---
description: Architecture and module boundary rules
applyTo: '**/*.ts'
---

<!-- Auto-generated by generate-copilot.prompt.md — do not remove this line -->

# Architecture Rules

## Module Structure

Each feature module lives under `apps/api/src/<feature>/` and contains:

- `<feature>.module.ts` — NestJS module declaration
- `<feature>.resolver.ts` — GraphQL resolver (queries and mutations)
- `<feature>.service.ts` — Business logic, caching, data access
- `schemas/<entity>.schema.ts` — Mongoose schema + GraphQL ObjectType
- `dto/create-<entity>.input.ts` — Creation input type
- `dto/update-<entity>.input.ts` — Update input type (extends create via PartialType)

## Dependency Direction

```
Resolver → Service → Mongoose Model
                   → Cache Manager (CACHE_MANAGER)
```

### Allowed

- Resolvers may depend on their module's service only
- Services may depend on Mongoose models and CACHE_MANAGER
- Any module may inject global providers (ConfigService, CACHE_MANAGER)

### Forbidden

- Feature modules must NOT import other feature modules
- Resolvers must NOT contain business logic or direct database access
- Services must NOT depend on resolvers
- No circular dependencies between modules

## Naming Conventions

| File Type | Pattern                          | Example                    |
| --------- | -------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| Module    | `<feature>.module.ts`            | `items.module.ts`          |
| Resolver  | `<feature>.resolver.ts`          | `items.resolver.ts`        |
| Service   | `<feature>.service.ts`           | `items.service.ts`         |
| Schema    | `schemas/<entity>.schema.ts`     | `schemas/item.schema.ts`   |
| DTO       | `dto/<action>-<entity>.input.ts` | `dto/create-item.input.ts` |
| Test      | `<name>.spec.ts` (co-located)    | `items.service.spec.ts`    |

## Error Handling

- Use NestJS built-in exceptions (`NotFoundException`, `BadRequestException`)
- Throw exceptions in services, not resolvers
- Let NestJS exception filters handle response formatting

## Caching Pattern

Services implement cache-aside:

1. Check Redis cache first
2. On miss, query MongoDB
3. Cache the result with 60s TTL
4. On writes, invalidate relevant cache keys

## Registration

- Register Mongoose schemas via `MongooseModule.forFeature()` in the feature module
- Register resolver and service as providers in the feature module
- Import the feature module in `AppModule`
